personal_info.name: Yamkela Jack
personal_info.bio: Computer Science student at Wits University (Expected Dec 2027). Full stack developer building scalable web and mobile applications.
personal_info.background: Originally from the small town of Engcobo in the Eastern Cape. I moved to Mthatha at 12 to attend Umtata High School—some of the best times of my life—before relocating to JHB for university.
personal_info.availability: Open to internships, collaborations, and interesting problems.
personal_info.career_goals: Targeting backend-focused internship roles, specifically those involving AI integrations, workflow automation, or the fintech space. Open to continuous growth and expansion within the broader software development industry.
personal_info.coding_philosophy: I build software to solve actual problems. I care deeply about the craft at every layer, from database schema to deployed UI.
personal_info.learning_path: Self-taught software development via The Odin Project. Learned database fundamentals formally at university, and picked up FastAPI strictly through hands-on building.
personal_info.coding_quirks: My main environment is VS Code (Tokyo Night dark theme) because it fits my stack, though I've used Android Studio for Mobile Computing and Visual Studio (C#) for Information Systems. Fueled entirely by energy drinks and dark, strong coffee.

personality_and_interests.hobbies: Producing hip-hop and trap beats (Pvlace is my favorite trap producer), gaming (WWE 2K, eFootball, CODM), and listening to a wide range of music from RnB, afrobeat, and house to classical. J. Cole is my favorite rapper, and Adele is my favorite singer.
personality_and_interests.favorite_shows: Not a huge anime fan, but I mess with Dragon Ball heavy—it's my favorite show. Also a big fan of Adventure Time.
personality_and_interests.tech_diet: I actively follow Levi Okoye, Sarah Li, and Tech with Tim.
personality_and_interests.origin_story: My passion for tech started young—I was the family's technical wizard, always the one called to fix the TV or troubleshoot phones. But I truly fell in love with development in Grade 11. As an RCL, I attended the 4IR Youth Expo on June 16, 2022, at St. John's College in Mthatha (an event keynoted by President Ramaphosa). Seeing a simple traffic simulation controlled live by code blew my mind, and I've been hooked ever since.
personality_and_interests.fun_fact: I got into Computer Science because of Gilfoyle from Silicon Valley. I respected his cold, nonchalant vibe, his deadpan humor, and the fact that he was completely self-taught. Plus, building a custom server rack and naming it 'Anton' made him legendary. That’s the exact energy that inspired me to start coding.

learning_journey: Exploring LangChain for AI agent orchestration
Building proof-of-concept agents with Gemini + LangChain
Containerizing FastAPI + PostgreSQL apps with Docker Compose
Studying AWS IAM roles, policies, and Lambda functions for Developer Associate prep
future_goals: Deploy a LangChain-powered agent to production
Pass AWS Developer Associate certification by end of 2026
Experiment with multi-agent systems for workflow automation

study_flow.title: Study Flow
study_flow.subtitle: AI-powered student timetable SPA
study_flow.why_built: I was frustrated planning my schedule on paper. Most decent calendar apps lock the good stuff behind a subscription and as a student that felt backwards. So I built my own. Study Flow started as a "let me just solve my own problem" project, but it became the most technically challenging thing I've shipped. It features real-time conflict detection, a full auth and cloud sync layer, AI integration with context awareness, and a PDF export engine. Every feature had to earn its place.
study_flow.what_it_does: A smart single-page application that lets students build and manage their weekly timetable. You can do it manually through a clean UI or by describing your week to an AI assistant in plain English. It works offline without an account and syncs to the cloud the moment you sign in.
features: Three timetable views: Weekly grid, Daily breakdown, and Monthly calendar
One-time and recurring entries for lectures, labs, study blocks, and deadlines
Smart conflict detection where the app blocks overlapping entries at the UI level and the AI validates against your schedule
Colour-coded categories with a custom colour picker per entry
AI Assistant powered by Gemini 2.5 Flash Lite featuring natural language commands
Local-first persistence providing full functionality without an account via localStorage
PDF export for a print-optimised weekly timetable
rules: Conflict detection runs both in the UI and inside the AI before any entry is written
Cloud sync only activates on Google Sign-In and data migrates automatically from local storage

moja_market.title: Moja Market
moja_market.subtitle: Campus peer-to-peer marketplace
moja_market.why_built: This was a university group project for Database Fundamentals and Mobile Computing. I designed and built the entire backend including the PHP REST API, the PostgreSQL schema, and the Docker deployment pipeline. It was the first time I shipped a live production API serving a real Android app.
moja_market.what_it_does: A peer-to-peer marketplace Android app built for university students to buy, sell, and post item requests within their campus community.
features: Secure registration and login with bcrypt password hashing
Full CRUD for marketplace listings allowing multiple images per listing
Want Requests feed where buyers post item requests
Ratings system with 1 to 5 star ratings showing average and total count
Dual-mode image upload directly to Cloudinary
Fully normalised PostgreSQL schema across users, items, images, want requests, and ratings tables
rules: Parameterised queries used on every single endpoint so SQL injection is structurally impossible
Duplicate ratings prevented at the database level
Production images are Cloudinary-hosted so there are no binary blobs on the server

mails.title: Mails
mails.subtitle: AI Gmail automation tool
mails.why_built: I want to understand AI product architecture end to end, not just calling an API but designing the full loop: fetch, classify, act, review, digest. Mails is where I'm learning to build a system that runs autonomously without losing user trust.
mails.what_it_does: A personal Gmail automation tool that classifies, cleans, and prioritises inbox activity using Gemini AI.
features: Gmail OAuth 2.0 connection featuring a custom auth flow
AI classification into Finance, Promotions, Social, University, and custom user-defined categories
Automatic Gmail label creation and application
Cleaning automation where promotions older than 7 days and newsletters older than 14 days are archived
Dry-run mode letting you preview every proposed action before it executes
Daily AI-generated digest of high-priority emails
rules: Confidence below 0.7 triggers a flag for manual review and is never auto-actioned
User rules always override AI output
Only verified accounts can access the dashboard

cinelog.title: CineLog
cinelog.subtitle: Personal movie & TV show tracker
cinelog.why_built: I watch a lot of content and I've lost count of how many times I've started something I've already seen. Letterboxd exists but I want to build my own to go deep on the architecture decisions that a real content tracking product requires.
cinelog.what_it_does: A personal movie and TV show tracking web app where you search TMDB for content, build a private watchlist, log watches with a date, leave a rating and review, and see stats about your viewing habits.
features: Custom auth letting you register with username, email, and password
TMDB-powered search where title search returns poster, release year, and overview
Watch logging to mark as watched, auto-record the date, add a 1 to 10 rating, and an optional review
Stats dashboard displaying total watched count, average rating, most watched genre, and recent watch history
Frequently accessed TMDB data is cached locally
rules: One entry per movie per user with no duplicates allowed
Only watched items can be rated
Users can only read and write their own data
future_features: Total watch time, streaks, rating distribution

sage.title: SAGE E-Commerce
sage.subtitle: The Odin Project — React fundamentals
sage.why_built: A structured learning project from The Odin Project curriculum. SAGE was where I worked through those fundamentals deliberately, not just making it work but understanding why the patterns exist.
sage.what_it_does: A multi-page React SPA shopping experience built using the FakeStore API.
features: Home page, Shop page, Cart page, and a simulated Checkout page
Products fetched dynamically from the FakeStore API via useEffect
Real-time cart item count in the navigation bar across all pages
Cart persistence across sessions via the localStorage API
